title: Graceful Partial Compliance
goal: Make startup and compact operating surfaces easier for generic agents to discover and cheaper to use.
Make package bypass, missing planning residue, and lower-trust closeout visible enough that reviewers can detect weak compliance quickly.
non_goals: Do not require full package adoption before any value appears.
Do not turn partial-compliance signals into a punitive workflow engine.
Do not bury startup routing under more prose or a second front door.

intent.outcome: Harden startup discoverability, compact core-surface cost, preflight takeover robustness, and lower-trust closeout visibility so generic agents can use the package without full doctrine reconstruction.
intent.constraints: Keep startup cheap, keep core surfaces compact, preserve repo-owned boundaries, and make likely bypass visible rather than silently acceptable.
intent.latitude: Sequence the lane through an audit-first slice that proves where startup/compliance still fails before broadening implementation.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the slice would require replacing the current startup model wholesale or widening into unrelated planning-chain work.

execution.next_step: Identify the highest-friction default startup ambiguity, tighten the cheapest startup entry surface around it, and add focused startup-discoverability tests.
execution.proof: Preflight-first takeover recovery and compact lower-trust visibility are both landed. The next proof is that a generic agent can notice and follow the default startup path more cheaply from the repo front door without broad repo scavenging.

invariants: AGENTS.md remains a thin repo-owned router into compact workspace surfaces.
Startup guidance stays cheaper than broad rereads.
Likely bypass or weak compliance remains visible as trust and review state, not hidden as normal operation.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make package startup and compact operating surfaces easy enough for generic agents to discover and use, while making bypass and low-trust closeout visible to stronger reviewers

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue until a generic agent can follow startup routing cheaply and bypass/weak-closeout incidents reliably surface in review state

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: The takeover-robustness slice for issue #277 is landed, and the lower-trust visibility slice for issue #256 is now landed too: report exposes a compact closeout-trust summary and the default text report surfaces lower-trust state without requiring a full scan of generic findings.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: broad doctrine compression beyond the audit findings, machine-first planning-chain extraction, and local-only integration-area work.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Lower-trust visibility is now much cheaper in report, which removes one major blind spot. The next remaining graceful-compliance gap is at the front door: generic agents still need the default startup entry path to feel more obvious and decisive than ad hoc repo reading.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: `agentic-workspace report` now exposes top-level closeout trust, the default text report shows lower-trust state directly, and focused workspace CLI tests pass for both JSON and text reporting.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: prove that the default entry path is easier to notice and follow without deep product-specific knowledge.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: tighten the cheapest startup/front-door surface under issue #254, then retest generic-agent discoverability before widening to broader package-surface cost work.

intent_interpretation.literal request: prepare a full execplan for the second roadmap lane to reduce weak-agent implementation failures
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: bound the graceful-partial-compliance lane into a planner-owned sequence that makes generic-agent startup and trust failures visible before they cause misleading implementation residue
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: start with an audit-first slice anchored in current startup and trust surfaces, then route the top failures into bounded fixes rather than attempting a broad compliance rewrite immediately
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject work that claims compliance improvement without reducing startup cost or without making lower-trust/bypass state more visible in compact review surfaces

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section startup --format json; uv run agentic-workspace config --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --format json; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "startup or summary or report or trust"; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before replacing the startup contract, rewriting broad planning storage, or redesigning unrelated package command structure
execution_bounds.stop before touching: vendor-specific integrations, machine-first sidecar storage internals, or runtime-posture execution policy outside compliance signaling

stop_conditions.stop when: the audit findings stop being bounded and start implying a broad workflow-substrate redesign
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: fixes require changing the repo-owned startup adapter contract beyond thin routing
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the work becomes generic docs cleanup or global UX polish without concrete startup/trust proof
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: compact surfaces still cannot expose bypass or lower-trust closeout after targeted changes

context_budget.live working set: startup defaults/config/summary/report outputs, AGENTS.md router contract, trust/lower-trust reporting surfaces, focused CLI tests, current lane issues
context_budget.recoverable later: older product-compression history and archived startup lanes unless a specific prior proof is needed
context_budget.externalize before shift: the audit findings, the ranked failure list, and the exact compact proof surfaces that should show lower-trust state
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace defaults --section startup --format json; agentic-workspace report --target . --format json
context_budget.tiny resumability note: start with an intent-validation audit, not with broad docs edits; prove cheap startup and visible lower-trust state
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after the audit artifact is written, after any startup contract tweak, or before handing a fix slice to a weaker worker

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: make generic-agent startup easier and weak compliance easier to detect
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: startup stays thin; compact surfaces remain primary; lower-trust state must become more visible, not more inferential
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: the audit format, the smallest proof queries, and whether one or two bounded fix slices follow first
delegated_judgment.escalate when: a worker needs to redefine the startup contract, claim full compliance without evidence, or widen into planning-chain redesign

references.label: make core package surfaces easier for generic agents
surface lower-trust signals when package use was likely bypassed
surface lower-trust signals when package use was likely bypassed
make the default entry path easier for generic agents to discover and follow

active_milestone.scope: Use the cheapest startup/front-door surfaces to make the default entry path more obvious and easier to follow after the trust-visibility slice closed.
active_milestone.optional_deps: gh issue refresh before implementation if #251/#253/#254 changed materially

immediate_next_action: Identify the highest-friction default startup ambiguity, tighten the cheapest entry surface around it, and add focused startup-discoverability tests for issue #254.
invariants: AGENTS.md stays a thin startup adapter.
Compact startup and planning surfaces remain cheaper than raw rereads.
Lower-trust and bypass behavior must remain visible to reviewers.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Startup questions should be answerable from defaults/config/summary/report before deeper docs are opened.
Lower-trust closeout must be visible in compact review surfaces, not only in long-form logs.
Audit-first is the default first step for this lane, because recent failures show hidden trust gaps as much as startup gaps.
open_questions_to_close: Which startup question is still easiest for a generic agent to miss from the repo front door: the entrypoint file, the first compact query, or the active-work routing step?
Which startup surface should carry the cheapest stronger ordering signal: AGENTS.md, startup report, or startup defaults?
Can the entry-path improvement stay bounded to one front-door surface and one focused test cut, or does it need a paired compact-query tweak too?
validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section startup --format json
uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "startup or summary or report or trust"
completion_criteria: The highest-signal startup discoverability failure is fixed or explicitly bounded for the next slice.
Compact surfaces show lower-trust or likely-bypass state clearly enough for a strong reviewer to detect it cheaply.
Focused tests cover both startup routing and lower-trust reporting.
The lane leaves a reusable audit-first pattern for future compliance incidents.

execution_run.what happened: Completed the lower-trust closeout visibility slice: added a compact report closeout-trust summary, surfaced it in the default text report, and kept the signal bounded to existing planning evidence.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "report_surfaces_compact_lower_trust_closeout_summary or report_text_surfaces_compact_lower_trust_closeout_summary or report_surfaces_planning_intent_validation_findings or report_surfaces_finished_work_inspection_findings"; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q
execution_run.result for continuation: issue #256 is ready to close; the lane should continue with default entry-path discoverability under issue #254.
execution_run.next step: implement the cheapest startup/front-door discoverability cut

finished_run_review.proof status: satisfied for the lower-trust report slice
finished_run_review.config compliance: respected checked-in and local config for the bounded lower-trust report slice
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low for the closed lower-trust slice; remaining risk sits in startup discoverability

proof_report.validation proof: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "report_surfaces_compact_lower_trust_closeout_summary or report_text_surfaces_compact_lower_trust_closeout_summary or report_surfaces_planning_intent_validation_findings or report_surfaces_finished_work_inspection_findings"`; `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q`
proof_report.proof achieved now: The repo now exposes compact lower-trust closeout state directly in report JSON and in the default human-readable report, making likely package bypass or missing planning residue visibly less trustworthy by default.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Focused report tests pass, the broader workspace CLI suite passes, and issue #256 acceptance criteria are satisfied by the shipped closeout-trust summary behavior.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: prepare a full execplan that lets a strong planner steer the graceful-partial-compliance lane safely
intent_satisfaction.was original intent fully satisfied?: yes for planning preparation, no for lane implementation
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: the lane now has an explicit audit-first plan with bounded validation and trust-signaling criteria

closure_check.slice status: preflight and lower-trust slices complete; lane remains active
closure_check.why this decision is honest: issues #277 and #256 are complete, but the broader graceful-partial-compliance lane still lacks a cheaper, more obvious default startup entry path and cheaper front-door package consumption.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan, the lane record in state.toml, and the next active slice under issue #254
closure_check.reopen trigger: revisit if the startup discoverability slice exposes a still-cheaper front-door cut or if live issue evidence reorders the lane

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Landed the lower-trust report visibility slice and selected default entry-path discoverability as the next bounded lane step.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: focused lower-trust report tests and the full workspace CLI suite
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Issue #256 can now close against the shipped closeout-trust report behavior and test proof.
